Stage 01

Safety Documentation

Your AI policy, governance, and human readiness — drafted in two weeks.

Standard engagement. Timeline and scope adapt to your situation.

2 Weeks · $5,000

Why this stage matters

Organizations spend months arguing in circles about artificial intelligence. Committees are formed, abstract risks are debated, and momentum dies. Safety Documentation cuts through this paralysis by providing a definitive, structural baseline for what is allowed, what is prohibited, and who is responsible.

We draft the entire suite in two weeks. You don't need endless discovery; you need a starting point. Once these guardrails are codified, the real work of testing tools and transforming workflows can begin securely.

Before you start

You need executive buy-in for establishing baseline AI rules and a designated point of contact for review cycles.

You're finished when

The full documentation suite is delivered, reviewed, and signed off by key stakeholders, ready for immediate operational deployment.

Five Pillars of Safety.

The Structural Defense

policy

Acceptable Use

Defining explicitly what is permitted and what is strictly prohibited. The baseline document for institutional clarity.

shield_locked

Data Boundaries

Securing proprietary institutional knowledge from public ingestion. Establishing closed-loop architectural perimeters.

visibility

Human Oversight

Mandating human-in-the-loop protocols for all externally facing outputs. The final arbiter of intent and tone.

record_voice_over

Voice and Trust

Calibrating models to reflect institutional tone while ensuring total transparency about machine generation with constituents.

balance

Ethical Guardrails

Aligning output with core theological or institutional values. Preventing bias and enforcing objective operational integrity.

analytics

Take the AI Assessment

Evaluate Your Readiness arrow_forward

What Safety produces.

The Core Artifacts of Record
Policy Framework
A comprehensive, board-ready document outlining acceptable AI utilization across all departmental verticals.
Data Guardrails
Technical specifications defining precisely what internal data can be processed, mapped against privacy compliance.
Oversight Protocol
The established chain of command for reviewing AI-generated outputs before they reach public consumption.
Trust Manifesto
Public-facing verbiage communicating your institution's transparent stance on AI integration to your constituents.
Ethical Ledger
A documented rubric ensuring AI utilization aligns explicitly with the core values and mission of the organization.
Training Syllabus
The curriculum required to upskill staff on these new protocols, moving from shadow IT to sanctioned capability.
Implementation Roadmap
The sequential timeline mapping exactly how these artifacts are deployed and enforced across the next two quarters.

What’s included

  • check AI Use & Trust Charter
  • check Acceptable Use Policy (AUP)
  • check Prohibited Use Cases List
  • check Data Privacy & Handling Addendum
  • check Vendor Evaluation Matrix
  • check Employee AI Disclosure Form
  • check Incident Response Protocol (AI Specific)
  • check AI Committee Formation Guide
  • check Tool Approval Workflow
  • check Shadow IT Amnesty Memo
  • check Baseline Training Requirements
  • check Review & Revision Schedule
  • check Charter Adoption Checklist

Exemplar Architectural Space

Questions about Safety Documentation

Do we really need 14 documents?

Yes. Each serves a distinct operational, legal, or cultural purpose. A generalized "AI Policy" is too vague to enforce. This granular suite provides specific guidance for IT (Vendor Matrix), HR (Training Requirements), and staff (AUP).

Is this just legal boilerplate?

No. While legally informed, these documents are designed to be read, understood, and operationalized by your workforce. They are written in plain language to foster trust, not just mitigate liability.

Who needs to be involved?

Typically, a working group comprising leadership, IT/Security, HR, and Legal/Compliance. We facilitate the alignment sessions to ensure these distinct perspectives are synthesized into a coherent policy suite.

What happens next

Move from theory to practice.

Once governance is established and constraints are clear, the next step is safely testing the technology against your actual work. Sandbox Discovery is ready.

The cost of not doing this work.

Organizations avoiding formalized Safety are operating with extreme fragility. Shadow AI isn't merely a technological issue; it is a profound governance failure.

When staff adopt AI without organizational guidance and guardrails, proprietary or private data leaks into public models. AI slop is published. But most critically, community trust—the absolute currency of churches and non-profits—is compromised invisibly.
